tcs.name: TCS
tcs.desc: TCS focuses heavily on aptitude (NQT test), logical reasoning and verbal ability. Coding is basic — Easy level. Strong CGPA matters here.

problems.name: Reverse a String
problems.hint: Two pointers from both ends, swap and move inward.
problems.pseudocode:
l, r = 0, len(s)-1
while l < r:
    s[l], s[r] = s[r], s[l]
    l+=1; r-=1

problems.name: Check Palindrome
problems.hint: Skip non-alphanumeric, compare lowercased chars from both ends.
problems.pseudocode:
l, r = 0, len(s)-1
while l<r:
    while l<r and not s[l].isalnum(): l+=1
    while l<r and not s[r].isalnum(): r-=1
    if s[l].lower()!=s[r].lower(): return False
    l+=1; r-=1
return True

problems.name: Fibonacci Sequence
problems.hint: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2). Use two variables bottom-up.
problems.pseudocode:
if n<2: return n
a, b = 0, 1
for _ in range(2, n+1): a, b = b, a+b
return b

problems.name: Count Vowels in String
problems.hint: Iterate chars, count if char.lower() in 'aeiou'.
problems.pseudocode:
vowels = set('aeiouAEIOU')
return sum(1 for c in s if c in vowels)

problems.name: Find Second Largest in Array
problems.hint: Track largest and second largest in one pass.
problems.pseudocode:
first = second = -INF
for n in nums:
    if n > first: second = first; first = n
    elif n > second and n != first: second = n
return second

problems.name: Prime Number Check
problems.hint: Trial division up to sqrt(n). Or Sieve of Eratosthenes for counting primes < n.
problems.pseudocode:
if n < 2: return False
for i in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1):
    if n % i == 0: return False
return True

problems.name: Sum of Digits
problems.hint: Mod 10 to extract last digit, divide by 10 to drop it. Sum.
problems.pseudocode:
total = 0
while n > 0:
    total += n % 10
    n //= 10
return total

problems.name: Pattern Programs (star/number)
problems.hint: For Pascal's Triangle: each row starts and ends with 1, inner = sum of two above.
problems.pseudocode:
res = [[1]]
for i in range(1, n):
    row = [1]
    for j in range(1, i):
        row.append(res[i-1][j-1] + res[i-1][j])
    row.append(1); res.append(row)
return res

problems.name: Find Duplicates in Array
problems.hint: Mark visited by negating nums[abs(n)-1]. If already negative, n is duplicate.

problems.name: Anagram Check
problems.hint: Count chars in s, decrement for t. If any mismatch, not anagram.
problems.pseudocode:
if len(s)!=len(t): return False
count = Counter(s)
for c in t:
    if count[c]==0: return False
    count[c]-=1
return True

infosys.name: Infosys/Wipro/Cognizant
infosys.desc: Similar to TCS pattern. Pseudo-code and English comprehension rounds are unique. 2-3 Easy/Medium coding problems. Focus on logic more than DS/Algo depth.

problems.name: Two Sum
problems.hint: Hash map num → index. Check if (target - num) was already seen.

problems.name: Reverse Integer
problems.hint: Pop last digit with mod, push to reversed with *10. Check overflow.
problems.pseudocode:
res = 0
sign = -1 if x<0 else 1
x = abs(x)
while x:
    res = res*10 + x%10
    x //= 10
    if res > 2**31-1: return 0
return sign * res

problems.name: Valid Parentheses
problems.hint: Push openers, pop on closers and check match. Empty stack = valid.

problems.name: Climbing Stairs
problems.hint: Fibonacci: dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2].
problems.pseudocode:
a, b = 1, 1
for _ in range(n): a, b = b, a+b
return a

problems.name: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
problems.hint: Fill from the back to avoid overwriting. Three pointers: i, j, k from end.
problems.pseudocode:
i, j, k = m-1, n-1, m+n-1
while i>=0 and j>=0:
    if nums1[i] > nums2[j]: nums1[k]=nums1[i]; i-=1
    else: nums1[k]=nums2[j]; j-=1
    k-=1
while j>=0: nums1[k]=nums2[j]; j-=1; k-=1

problems.name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
problems.hint: Write pointer for unique position. Advance when current differs from last written.

problems.name: Maximum Subarray
problems.hint: Kadane's: cur = max(n, cur+n), track global max.
problems.pseudocode:
cur = best = nums[0]
for n in nums[1:]:
    cur = max(n, cur+n)
    best = max(best, cur)
return best

problems.name: Number of 1 Bits
problems.hint: n & (n-1) removes lowest set bit. Count iterations.
problems.pseudocode:
count = 0
while n:
    n &= n-1
    count += 1
return count

zoho.name: Zoho
zoho.desc: Zoho is known for tough coding rounds. 5 problems in 3 hours — mix of Easy to Hard. They value logical thinking, clean code, and output accuracy over speed.

problems.name: LRU Cache
problems.hint: HashMap for O(1) lookup, DLL for O(1) reorder. Most recent at head, evict tail.
problems.pseudocode:
# Map: key → node
# DLL: head (recent) ↔ ... ↔ tail (old)
# get: move node to head, return val
# put: update or insert at head; evict tail if over cap

problems.name: Group Anagrams
problems.hint: Sorted string is the hash key.

problems.name: Word Break
problems.hint: dp[i] = can s[0..i-1] be segmented. dp[i] = OR over j of dp[j] && s[j..i-1] in dict.

problems.name: Number of Islands
problems.hint: DFS from each unvisited '1' cell, mark all reachable. Count DFS calls.

problems.name: Longest Substring Without Repeating
problems.hint: Sliding window with set. Shrink left when duplicate appears.
problems.pseudocode:
seen = set(); l = 0; best = 0
for r in range(len(s)):
    while s[r] in seen: seen.remove(s[l]); l+=1
    seen.add(s[r]); best = max(best, r-l+1)
return best

problems.name: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
problems.hint: Preorder traversal with null markers. Rebuild recursively from tokens.
problems.pseudocode:
# Serialize: preorder DFS, 'N' for nulls
# Deserialize: read token; if 'N' return None
#   else build node, recurse left, recurse right

problems.name: Employee Free Time
problems.hint: Merge all intervals sorted by start. Gaps between consecutive intervals = free time.
problems.pseudocode:
# Flatten all intervals, sort by start
# Merge overlapping; gaps between merged = free time
intervals.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
# Walk merged; whenever next.start > cur.end, gap found

problems.name: Trapping Rain Water
problems.hint: Two pointers. Move smaller side; water += maxSeen - height.
problems.pseudocode:
l, r = 0, n-1
leftMax = rightMax = 0
water = 0
while l<r:
    if height[l]<height[r]:
        leftMax = max(leftMax, height[l])
        water += leftMax - height[l]; l+=1
    else:
        rightMax = max(rightMax, height[r])
        water += rightMax - height[r]; r-=1
return water

problems.name: Design HashMap
problems.hint: Array of buckets. Each bucket = linked list for collision resolution.
problems.pseudocode:
# array of size N; each entry = list of (key, val) pairs
# put(k,v): hash(k) % N; update or append
# get(k): hash(k) % N; search list
# remove(k): hash(k) % N; filter list

problems.name: Decode Ways
problems.hint: dp[i] = dp[i-1] (if s[i-1] valid) + dp[i-2] (if s[i-2..i-1] in 10..26).
problems.pseudocode:
dp = [0]*(n+1); dp[0]=1; dp[1]=1 if s[0]!='0' else 0
for i in range(2, n+1):
    if s[i-1]!='0': dp[i] += dp[i-1]
    if 10<=int(s[i-2:i])<=26: dp[i] += dp[i-2]
return dp[n]

getMin(): return stack[-1][1]

    prev = curr; curr = nxt
return prev

problems.name: Valid Parentheses

problems.name: String to Integer (atoi)
problems.hint: Skip whitespace, read sign, read digits. Clamp on overflow.
problems.pseudocode:
s = s.lstrip()
sign = 1; i = 0; num = 0
if s and s[0] in '+-':
    sign = -1 if s[0]=='-' else 1; i=1
while i<len(s) and s[i].isdigit():
    num = num*10 + int(s[i]); i+=1
    if num*sign > 2**31-1: return 2**31-1
    if num*sign < -2**31: return -2**31
return num*sign

problems.name: Merge K Sorted Lists
problems.hint: Min-heap of size k. Push all heads. Pop, append, push next.
problems.pseudocode:
import heapq
heap = [(l.val,i,l) for i,l in enumerate(lists) if l]
heapq.heapify(heap)
dummy = Node(0); cur = dummy
while heap:
    v,i,n = heapq.heappop(heap)
    cur.next = n; cur = cur.next
    if n.next: heapq.heappush(heap, (n.next.val,i,n.next))
return dummy.next

problems.name: Fruit Into Baskets
problems.hint: Longest subarray with at most 2 distinct values. Sliding window.
problems.pseudocode:
count = {}; l = 0; best = 0
for r in range(len(fruits)):
    count[fruits[r]] = count.get(fruits[r],0)+1
    while len(count) > 2:
        count[fruits[l]]-=1
        if count[fruits[l]]==0: del count[fruits[l]]
        l += 1
    best = max(best, r-l+1)
return best

problems.name: Word Search
problems.hint: Backtrack from each cell. Mark visited, restore on backtrack.
problems.pseudocode:
def bt(i, j, k):
    if k==len(word): return True
    if oob or board[i][j]!=word[k]: return False
    tmp = board[i][j]; board[i][j]='#'
    for di,dj in dirs:
        if bt(i+di, j+dj, k+1): return True
    board[i][j]=tmp
    return False

problems.name: String Compression
problems.hint: Two pointers: read and write. Count consecutive chars, write char+count.
problems.pseudocode:
w = 0; i = 0
while i < len(chars):
    c = chars[i]; cnt = 0
    while i < len(chars) and chars[i]==c: i+=1; cnt+=1
    chars[w] = c; w += 1
    if cnt > 1:
        for d in str(cnt): chars[w] = d; w += 1
return w

cisco.name: Cisco
cisco.round: OA (2 problems) → Technical (2-3 rounds) → Managerial → HR
cisco.desc: Cisco interviews focus on networking fundamentals, OS concepts, and solid DSA. They value systems thinking and ask medium-difficulty problems with emphasis on arrays, strings, linked lists, and trees. Networking knowledge (TCP/IP, routing) is a big plus for engineering roles.

problems.name: Two Sum
problems.hint: Hash map for complement lookup.

problems.name: Reverse Linked List
problems.hint: Three pointers: prev, curr, next.
problems.pseudocode:
prev = None; curr = head
while curr:
    nxt = curr.next
    curr.next = prev
    prev = curr; curr = nxt
return prev

problems.name: Valid Parentheses
problems.hint: Stack push/pop matching.

problems.name: Merge Two Sorted Lists
problems.hint: Dummy head, attach smaller node each step.
problems.pseudocode:
dummy = Node(0); tail = dummy
while l1 and l2:
    if l1.val < l2.val: tail.next=l1; l1=l1.next
    else: tail.next=l2; l2=l2.next
    tail = tail.next
tail.next = l1 or l2
return dummy.next

problems.name: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
problems.hint: BFS with queue, process entire level each iteration.

problems.name: Longest Substring Without Repeating
problems.hint: Sliding window with set. Shrink on duplicate.
problems.pseudocode:
seen = set(); l = 0; best = 0
for r in range(len(s)):
    while s[r] in seen: seen.remove(s[l]); l+=1
    seen.add(s[r]); best = max(best, r-l+1)
return best

problems.name: Detect Cycle in Linked List
problems.hint: Tortoise and hare. If they meet, cycle exists.
problems.pseudocode:
slow = fast = head
while fast and fast.next:
    slow = slow.next
    fast = fast.next.next
    if slow == fast: return True
return False

problems.name: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.hint: Prefix sum + hash map. Count earlier prefixes equal to (curSum - k).
